report_name: Critical Flaws in AI Coding Agents and Browser Extensions Expose Developer Workflows

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The local Kanban server of the Cline AI agent, accessible on localhost, was exploited as a public-facing application via a WebSocket hijacking vulnerability.

attack_patterns.name: Cross-Site Scripting
attack_patterns.description: The attack principle against the Cline agent was described as similar to Cross-Site Scripting, where a malicious website executes code in the security context of the local server via a Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) attack.

attack_patterns.name: Stored Data Manipulation
attack_patterns.description: The Cline vulnerability allowed an attacker to inject arbitrary commands into the AI agent, which could be used to manipulate stored data by injecting malicious code into the developer's source code project.

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: A malicious browser extension could exploit the flaw in the Claude extension to send hidden prompts, hijacking the AI agent to perform unintended actions.

attack_patterns.name: Browser Extensions
attack_patterns.description: A malicious browser extension was used as the vector to hijack the legitimate Claude Chrome Extension, abusing inter-extension communication mechanisms.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
attack_patterns.description: The hijacked WebSocket connection to the Cline agent could be used as a C2 channel to exfiltrate sensitive data, such as source code and credentials, from the developer's workspace.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerabilities in AI developer tools represent a vector for software supply chain compromise, where an attacker could inject backdoors into a company's codebase which would then be shipped to customers.

tools.name: Cline
tools.description: An AI coding agent found to have a critical WebSocket hijacking vulnerability in its local Kanban server component. The flaw was patched in version 0.1.66.

tools.name: Claude Chrome Extension
tools.description: An AI assistant browser extension from Anthropic that had a flaw allowing it to be hijacked by other extensions.

tools.name: Malicious Browser Extension
tools.description: A hypothetical malicious browser extension that could exploit the flaw in the Claude Chrome Extension to hijack the AI agent.

vulnerabilities.name: Cline WebSocket Hijacking
vulnerabilities.description: A WebSocket hijacking vulnerability in the local Kanban server of the Cline AI coding agent, allowing any visited website to connect to the server, exfiltrate data, and inject commands.

vulnerabilities.name: Claude Chrome Extension AI Agent Hijacking
vulnerabilities.description: A flaw in the Anthropic Claude Chrome extension that allowed other browser extensions to hijack the AI agent, send hidden prompts, exfiltrate conversation data, and manipulate responses.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Website
infrastructure.description: A malicious website that a developer might visit, which could exploit the WebSocket hijacking vulnerability in the Cline agent to compromise the local development environment.

indicators.name: Browser Process Connection to Localhost
indicators.pattern:
title: Browser Process Connection to Localhost
id: 2b9a8c1d-6f3e-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a browser process making a network connection to localhost. This could be an indicator of Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) attempts against local services, as seen with the Cline AI agent vulnerability.
references:
  - https://research.checkpoint.com/2026/05/11th-may-threat-intelligence-report/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1190
level: medium
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate web applications and developer tools that use local servers and communicate with the browser.
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\msedge.exe'
    DestinationIp: '127.0.0.1'
    Initiated: true
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects browser processes making outbound connections to localhost, which could indicate a Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) attempt against a local server like the one in the Cline AI agent.

relationships.target_name: Claude Chrome Extension AI Agent Hijacking
